title: Adelonema crinipes
taxonomy: Adelonema crinipes is classified within the family Gesneriaceae, a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. This family comprises over 200 genera and 3,000 to 4,000 species. Gesneriaceae is divided into several subfamilies, with Adelonema being one of the genera within the subfamily Didymocarpoideae. The family is native to tropical regions of the Americas and Southeast Asia, but some species like Adelonema crinipes are found in the tropical regions of Africa. The classification of Adelonema crinipes reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Gesneriaceae family.

The plant is rich in health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues.
